name: A Wangshan Walk to Remember
desc: At Huang the Third's invitation, you arrive at the legendary place where the treasure of the Guhua Clan is located, Wangshan Hall...

content.name: Scrolls and Sword Manuals of Guhua

content.text: Follow Huang the Third to the site of the trial
Learn about the rules for the trial
Follow Huang the Third to the site of the next trial
Learn about the rules for the trial
Speak with Huang the Third and go to the next location
Speak with Huang the Third and go to the next location
Step to the side and listen to Paimon's idea
Give Paimon's special dish to Huang the Third
At Huang the Third's invitation, you arrive at the legendary place where the treasure of the Guhua Clan is located, Wangshan Hall...

I behold your immaculate appearance, with eyes as bright as shining stars/your extraordinary beauty, a divinity in this mortal world, and know from this one glance that you nobly wander this land. Therefore, prithee tarry a while, as I make so bold to request your good name?
Not bad. You've got good eyes for some old guy!
Haha, your breathtaking confidence is a rarity. I have certainly not erred!
What in the world are you banging on about... You too, Traveler! Don't let a few compliments go to your head!
Ahem, anyway, Paimon at your service. He's/She's Traveler. And you are...?
Huh? Are you talking to me?
Precisely! I perceived your uncommon demeanor with one glance. You must also be an itinerant hero/heroine, are you not?
Er, dunno about any itinerant whatevers. But anyway, Paimon, at your service. He's/She's Traveler. And you are...?
Ah, yes. Where are my manners? Huang the Third of the Guhua Clan, at your service. Some people know me as the Cloudpiercing Arrow.
Cloudpiercing Arrow...? So you're a talented archer?
Haha, it was just a moniker bestowed on me in my youth. Pay it no mind.
Oh, so you're Huang the Third! The one they call the Cloudpiercing Arrow...
Hey, don't tell Paimon you've heard of this guy!?
Wait a minute, did you just say... Guhua Clan?
Oh? Are you familiar with our clan's name?
Now, where did I hear about it...?
Excellent, excellent! I thought the Clan's fortunes had waned so much that its name was now all but forgotten. I did not expect you to be so well-informed.
To be frank, I approached you because I am inviting select interested parties to visit Wangshan Hall, our Clan stronghold, and explore history itself.
If you have nowhere pressing to be, how about coming with me to see the Hall? I promise you won't be disappointed!
You're turning your stronghold into a tourist attraction?
Err... Well, our disciples are scattered all over Liyue. As the Hall is currently deserted, what better time to invite guests?
What's more, it contains treasures never before seen...
I see... Alas! Would it not be a shame if the treasures housed still within Wangshan Hall will remain thus neglected, deep within the mountains...! A shame. Such a shame!
Quite so! Grand Master Guhua secretly established 12 Secret Chambers within the Hall upon the Clan's founding, in which he placed treasures now lost to the world.

Wangshan Hall is hewn from the mountain stone itself, and its structure is complex and intricate. Over millennia, only a handful of these hidden chambers have been discovered. The locations of many others are unknown even to us disciples.
I sense something special about you. Perchance you might stumble across one of Grand Master Guhua's hidden chambers during your visit to this historical site!
(This has to be a gimmick to sucker tourists in...)
(Surely no one could fall for this...)
12 Secret Chambers!? For real!? Traveler, Traveler! We've gotta see this! Y'know, and those secret Guhua Clan treasures!
Hey, what's with that look? Aren't you tempted at all? We'll be rich if we find that forgotten treasure!
Oh! That reminds Paimon... Isn't Xingqiu a member of the Guhua Clan? Maybe he once trained there!
Fine then. Lead the way, Paimon.
Hehe, awesome! Paimon just knew that you'd be interested!
Hang on, how much does it cost to tour Wangshan Hall?
Cost? Haha, surely you jest! We are honorable disciples of the Guhua Clan, and wish only to invite those of character to come visit. We are not brash, swindling charlatans!
I'm not asking for a penny! How about it, then? Would you care to proceed to Wangshan Hall to explore our illustrious history?
Great, that's just what I was thinking!
Since it's free, we might as well go take a look...
Wonderful! Well, without further ado, I'll go plan a sightseeing route for you both! Please, simply follow the map straight to Wangshan Hall!
Huang the Third takes his leave...
Great! Let's go soon, Traveler! Paimon can't wait!
(No... How can you take those antiques out without maintaining them!? What if...)
(Hey, you don't get it... If I did that, and people saw that the paint was new, would they still be willing to come play... I mean, come train!? The sense of history is... whatchamacallit... Hey!)
(...And anyway, the two of us don't have that much spare cash...)
Oh, oh... Welcome! I see you've made it to our stronghold — Wangshan Hall!
Haha, worry not! This is my junior brother, known in our circles as Wen the Fourth, and by the people as the Snowstriding Sword.
However, Wangshan Hall is, in the end, very important to us. Custom dictates, therefore, that outsiders who wish to enter must first pass two trials.
Put another way, we first wish to see just how skilled you are!
(Well, just look what gullible fish just got on the hook...)
Of course, of course! These two tests will certainly be a walk in the park for your illustrious selves!
Haha, no need to worry! These two tests will certainly be a walk in the park for your illustrious selves!
It's just that these are the rules of our clan, and we can't simply change them arbitrarily. Please forgive me.
Boo... Nothing for it. We do want the treasure from those 12 Secret Chambers...
The 12 Secret Chambers, eh? Haven't you told these guests a lot? Did you count those chambers specifically...?
So, what exactly do I need to do?
It's very simple. Please, follow me!
Outsiders who seek to enter Wangshan Hall must pass two trials. This rule has existed since ancient times.
However... Forget it, if my senior thinks it's all right, then I have nothing to say about it. Anyway, there's nothing to worry about. Anyone can pass the trials as they are right now.
...You will understand soon enough. Go on, don't let me keep you. My senior is waiting.
We'll be on our way then. Goodbye!
Go on, my senior is waiting.
According to Huang the Third, anyone wishing to enter Wangshan Hall who is not a disciple must first pass two trials...
Follow Huang the Third to the site of the trial
Learn about the rules for the trial
Behold the Plumwatcher Piles, passed down by Grand Master Guhua!

It was upon these stones that Grand Master Guhua practiced back in the day, diligently cultivating the technique to become a true master! Over there, the ancient trees were also planted by our founder's hand...
Wow, they're all at crazy angles, and some are even broken in half...
It's probably due to years of disrepair and neglect...
Ahem... Of course not! It's merely to increase the difficulty of the training, nothing more! After all, there won't be neat stone piles to step on in a real battle, will there?
Nobody would step on one of these things in a real fight either!
...In any case! For the first trial, I ask that you step on the stone piles in the order I specify within the allotted time, Traveler. Show me what you've got!
C'mon, Traveler, you've got this! If you wanna get the treasure... Ahem, Paimon means, if you wanna pass the trial, you've gotta be willing to put in the effort!
The additional dialogue appears during the trial if the player successfully climbed onto the poles.
Simply incredible! This way next, please.
Not bad! Your skill is truly extraordinary, Young Hero/Young Heroine!
Okay! Almost there, one last step!
The additional dialogue appears during the trial if the player fell into the water.
Wah! Into the drink... Th—That didn't count! Again!
Haha, there are no do-overs in life! Young Hero/Young Heroine‍, fret not. Whether we rise or fall, it is all but part of the trial! What matters is to press on.
The additional dialogue appears during the trial if the player don't move to the next pole.
Hey, Traveler, stop zoning out! At this rate, we'll fail the trial!
There are 5 scenarios for the following dialogue:
The player completed the Trial successfully.
Such extraordinary technique! You moved like a swallow in flight, with not a droplet of water staining your wings. Such unparalleled grace is truly worthy of a hero!
Hmm... Does using a wind glider count as a technique?
Haha, it looks like you still have much to learn about traditional martial arts, child.
I ask you this: When a wandering hero strides out and brings bandits and thieves alike to justice, was it their skill that deserves credit, or their sword's?
Uh... The hero's skill, of course!
Correct! Even the sharpest blade is ineffective in the hands of one who knows not how to wield it.
It is by this same reasoning that Traveler could use his/her wind glider so superbly. This is the kind of mastery we strive for in Guhua martial arts!
Hmm, that does make sense. But it also doesn't make sense at all...
With that, let me congratulate you on acing the first trial, Traveler! For the second trial, we must proceed to the practice arena. Please come with me!
That's right, and Paimon will be cheering you on!
The player fell into the water.
*sniff* I fell because I wasn't looking...
It's all because these poles are too dang slippery!
Come on, be careful! *sigh* It's a good thing there's only water under these poles... Had it been solid ground, you probably would have gotten hurt.
Such extraordinary technique! You moved with remarkable finesse, like a swallow in flight. Such unparalleled grace is truly worthy of a hero!
Huh? What are you talking about? Traveler fell right into the water! Good thing he/she can swim...
Haha, it looks like you still have much to learn about traditional martial arts, child.
You see... This trial is meant to test visitors against these stone piles.
But how could a few stone piles gauge the limits of your extraordinary technique? Doubtless, you intentionally went off the beaten path and dropped into the water to display your true skill...

You fell exactly above the array's core, and as you swam back ashore, you perfectly undid said array. Others may not understand these profundities, but how could it ever escape me, the Cloudpiercing Arrow?
Eh... Are you two being serious?
With that, let me congratulate you on acing the first trial, Traveler! For the second trial, we must proceed to the practice arena. Please come with me!
No, but you didn't win either!
The player ran out of time.
Such extraordinary technique! You moved like a swallow in flight, with not a droplet of water staining your wings. Such unparalleled grace is truly worthy of a hero!
Wait! How the heck did you come to that conclusion!? It wasn't completed at all!
Haha, it looks like you still have much to learn about traditional martial arts, child.
You see... This trial is meant to test visitors against these stone piles.
Let me ask you a question. Did your companion lose his/her footing and fall into the water?
And that is where the secret lies! If the goal was only to follow the rules and move about on the wooden piles, then it would have been a piece of cake with Traveler's agility.
But the great do not allow themselves to be bound by rules!
Just clearing the first few wooden piles was enough to prove Traveler's skill, and standing firm afterward demonstrated uncompromising will! An admirable combination of integrity and skill, indeed!
Eh... Are you two being serious?
Just staying out of the water is considered a success?
With that, let me congratulate you on acing the first trial, Traveler! For the second trial, we must proceed to the practice arena. Please come with me!
No, but you didn't win either!
The player did not attempt the Trial and ran out of time.
Hey! Why are you just standing there?
Oh, come on, how could you get distracted at a time like this?
Excellent! Only a true hero like you could have immediately seen through the true essence of this trial!
If the goal was only to follow the rules and move about on the stone piles, then I'm sure Traveler's agility would have made short work of it.
But Traveler stood still on the spot. Now, why is that?
It's, uh, just really high up...
You say that, but Paimon doesn't think you've ever been scared of heights before...
Excellent! Only a true hero like you could have immediately seen through the true essence of this trial!
If the goal was only to follow the rules and move about on the stone piles, then I'm sure Traveler's agility would have made short work of it.
But Traveler stood still on the spot. Now, why is that?
Haha, it looks like you still have much to learn about traditional martial arts, child.
You see... This trial is meant to test visitors against these stone piles.

As our predecessors once said, the prudent should foresee danger and take precautions. This is exactly what they meant! And Traveler would rather give up on the trial than betray this principle. Truly admirable!
Eh... Are you two being serious?
With that, let me congratulate you on acing the first trial, Traveler! For the second trial, we must proceed to the practice arena. Please come with me!
The player drowned in the water.
*sniff* I fell because I wasn't looking...
Well, now I can say I've definitely drunk enough!
Hey, that's not something to be proud of! You could've gotten hurt there! Seriously, take better care of yourself!
Excellent! Who knew you could hold your breath underwater for so long and still seem so alive after being fished out... No, you're completely fine! A true hero indeed!
What do you mean, holding his/her breath underwater!? He/She clearly swallowed a ton of water down there!
Haha, it looks like you still have much to learn about traditional martial arts, child.
I ask you this: When a wandering hero strides out and brings bandits and thieves alike to justice, was it their skill that deserves credit, or their sword's?
Uh... The hero's skill, of course... Wait! What has that go to do with anything?
Everything! Everyone knows that holding your breath in the open air is part of the basics while holding your breath underwater is the supreme technique!
As they say, the form of martial arts is fluid, and anything can be a sword. Even flowers and leaves can harm others. Traveler's mastery, therefore, is truly admirable!
Uh... Nothing you said seems to have any connection with that...
You see... This trial is meant to test visitors against these stone piles.
Since you've displayed an extraordinary ability to hold your breath, you've been tested sufficiently and sufficient!
This makes no sense at all!
With that, let me congratulate you on acing the first trial, Traveler! For the second trial, we must proceed to the practice arena. Please come with me!
What are you so happy about!?
Follow Huang the Third to the site of the next trial
Optional Dialogue: Talk to Wen the Fourth
The alternative dialogue appears if the player completed the Trial successfully.
But you need more practice if you seek to further yourself.
I saw the way you moved just now. I won't comment on the details, but it was pretty good overall. I'm certain it will become a great weapon in your arsenal if you work hard on it.
Hah... I may say that, but even now, it far surpasses anything my senior is capable of.
The alternative dialogue appears if the player failed the Trial.
You seem hesitant to say something...
Hey! So what do you think about my skill!?
Forget it... If my senior thinks it's all right, then I have nothing to say about it. So don't ask me.
I never expected our clan to end up in such a state... Yet there is nothing that can be done...
Learn about the rules for the trial
Back then, the reputation of the Guhua Clan was known throughout Liyue. But what was it based on? It was based on the spear and sword skills developed right here!
The dual mastery of polearm and sword we are known for is the result of arduous practice, day after day!
(Is this where Xingqiu used to train then?)
This is the second trial, the Trial of Spear and Sword! Just one moment while I get the training equipment out...
Huang the Third produces a bunch of dusty bamboo dummies from somewhere...
Phew, I'm exhausted... Okay! Anyway, would you please display your skills on these dummies?
Sure, but what exactly should I do?
Surely you don't expect me to knock all these dummies down in two seconds flat or something...?
Haha, surely you jest! Who could train in such a ludicrous fashion?
Take a look. Some of these dummies are dressed in blue while others are dressed in red.
The battlefield may turn on a dime. You must be able to distinguish friend from foe. To clear this trial, defeat all the dummies dressed in red within the allotted time!
If you strike a dummy dressed in blue, you lose. Stay alert, Traveler!
Ooh, Paimon gets it! So, you have to attack the dummies in red without hitting a single dummy in blue...
Great! That's the spirit! In this case, let's get started!
Huh? Let's see... Paimon thinks he's saying you can only hit... the ones in blue, right? But you can't hit any of the ones in red...
You've got it the wrong way around! You must attack all the dummies dressed in red without hitting a single one that's dressed in blue!
Could you say that one more time?
Eh? It's... You have to hit all those dressed in... Ahem, anyway, you get the idea!
Oh, I see! The second trial is a tongue twister...
It really isn't! Even though Paimon's a little confused... At least, just hit all the dummies of one color, which is... red? Probably...
The additional dialogue appears during the trial if the player hit a blue-clothed dummy.
Eh? Looks like a blue-clothed dummy was hit...
...Wait, is it red or blue that should be hit? Let me think for a moment... Ah, forget it! Just hit all of one color, that'll be good enough!
The additional dialogue appears during the trial if the player does not attack a dummy.
Hey, Traveler, stop zoning out! At this rate, we'll fail the trial!
The additional dialogue appears during the trial if the player hit a blue-clothed dummy and time is running out.
Waaah! Oh no, Paimon remembered! Huang the Third wanted us to hit all the red-clothed dummies, but we accidentally hit a blue one!
There are 5 scenarios for the following dialogue:
The player completed the Trial successfully.
Woohoo! That means we've successfully cleared the stage, right?
It was a piece of cake!
What a chore... There shouldn't be anything else after this, right?
A—Amazing! You effortlessly defeated all the targets with outstanding speed and accuracy! Even among our disciples, few can ever hope to rival you!
Are you the master of another clan, here on some secret visit?
Indeed. I'm, in truth, the master of the Paimon Clan...
Oh! You're the Pai... Uh... So you're the master of another clan! It is an honor to meet you, an honor indeed!
What in the world... That's enough nonsense, Traveler! Identifying targets is child's play to combat veterans like us!
Nah... Identifying targets is just child's play to combat veterans like us!
Hahaha! I would expect nothing less from someone as skilled as you, Traveler!
Anyway, as you've passed two trials, you've naturally earned the right to visit Wangshan Hall. Please come with me!
Hehe! Wangshan Hall's treasures, here we come!
The player destroyed a blue-clothed dummy.
Whoa, we accidentally hit one of those dummies in blue!
There goes the stage, and the treasure with it...
It's all because this platform is too dang slippery!
A—Amazing! You saw through such a meticulous disguise with a single glance!
The dummies in blue Traveler just hit were the spies hiding among us!
Haha! Don't you understand, little one? As they say, you never know what lurks on the inside. How could they be distinguished by appearance alone?
Yet, Traveler saw through their appearance and peered into their very nature! Such perception truly is the pinnacle of skill!
Wait! Just wait! Dummies don't have a "nature"! And you, Traveler! Stop looking so smug! It was just an accident, wasn't it!?
Are you even listening to yourself right now...
Anyway, as you've passed two trials, you've naturally earned the right to visit Wangshan Hall. Please come with me!
Th—That's a pass? Well... As long as it gets us in... Look out, Treasures of Guhua, 'cause here we come!
The player destroyed a blue-clothed dummy without destroying any red-clothed dummies.
Whoa, we accidentally hit one of those dummies in blue!
We also didn't manage to hit all the ones in red... There goes the stage, and also the treasure...
It's all because this platform is too dang slippery!
A—Amazing! You saw through such a meticulous disguise with a single glance!
The dummies in blue Traveler just hit were the impostors hiding among us! And the dummies in red that were spared? They were our undercover agents!
Hmm... and how could you tell?
Haha! Don't you understand, little one? As they say, you never know what lurks on the inside. How could they be distinguished by appearance alone?
Yet, Traveler saw through their appearance and peered into their very nature! Such perception truly is the pinnacle of skill!
Wait! Just wait! Dummies don't have a "nature"! And you, Traveler! Stop looking so smug! It was just an accident, wasn't it!?
Are you even listening to yourself right now...
Anyway, as you've passed two trials, you've naturally earned the right to visit Wangshan Hall. Please come with me!
Th—That's a pass? Well... As long as it gets us in... Look out, Treasures of Guhua, 'cause here we come!
The player ran out of time but did not attack any blue-clothed dummies.
There goes the stage, and the treasure with it...
It's all because this platform is too dang slippery!
A—Amazing! You saw through such a meticulous disguise with a single glance!
The dummies in red that Traveler just spared were our undercover agents! Hitting them by accident would be no different from hitting ourselves on the head!
U—Undercover agents? How could you tell?
Haha! Don't you understand, little one? As they say, you never know what lurks on the inside. How could they be distinguished by appearance alone?
Yet, Traveler saw through their appearance and peered into their very nature! Such perception truly is the pinnacle of skill!
Wait! Just wait! Dummies don't have a nature! And you, Traveler! Stop looking so smug! You just failed to finish them off, right?
Are you even listening to yourself right now...
Anyway, as you've passed two trials, you've naturally earned the right to visit Wangshan Hall. Please come with me!
Th—That's a pass? Well... As long as it gets us in... Look out, Treasures of Guhua, 'cause here we come!
The player ran out of time and did not attack any dummies.
Traveler! Why are you just standing there?
There goes the stage, and the treasure with it...
I lost concentration for a sec, that's all...
Wow, Paimon didn't know you could zone out even at a time like this...
Huh? What did you just say?
I have no idea which ones I'm supposed to hit...
Uh... Well, you do have a point... How are we supposed to know which ones to hit between all the dummies of different colors...
Excellent! I've never met someone so understanding and righteous in all my life! I truly admire you!
Wait! There's nothing to admire about that! Traveler just stood there on the spot!
Haha! You fail to see beyond the surface of things!
Traveler's skill is more than enough to instantly take down all those dummies in red. But that is not the Way.
Some swords in this world take life, and some give it. Taking life is easy, and giving it, hard. And the greatest principle in martial arts is to not kill!

While they may be dummies made of bamboo, they still symbolize living beings. Traveler's compassion and decision to feign distraction just to protect the innocent, even at the cost of failing the trial... It's truly admirable!
What the heck is this nonsense... Hey, Traveler! Stop looking so smug! You were just distracted, weren't you!?
Haha! There's no need for humility! I'm fully capable of distinguishing your true intent!
Anyway, as you've passed two trials, you've naturally earned the right to visit Wangshan Hall. Please come with me!
Th—That's a pass? Well... As long as it gets us in... Look out, Treasures of Guhua, 'cause here we come!
Optional Dialogue: Talk to Wen the Fourth
If you have anything to ask about the Guhua Clan... Feel free to ask me. As for what my senior said... Don't believe everything he says.
Wangshan Hall is the main house of the Guhua Clan and where Grand Master Guhua attained enlightenment.
Now, it is in ruins and unusable... Besides my senior and I, no one else remains to guard this place.
Even our current Master merely visits occasionally.
The Guhua Clan was once known as the Guhua Brotherhood. We looked up to the chivalry of Grand Master Guhua and traveled the world with our swords and spears... Our mastery of both was known to all.
Sigh... But as time passed, the clan's glory was no more...
Exactly. The Guhua Clan has three ultimate techniques — Light Piercer, Rain Cutter, and Life Ender. The three were known as the Three Guhua Greats.
Life Ender is a unique fusion of sword and spear, and it is why the Guhua Clan is known to have mastery over both.
It is said that Life Ender could slice wind, split waves, and destroy mountains. Unfortunately, I never had the chance to witness it for myself.

Later on, during the time of the 11th Master... Yingmin, the one known as the Piercing Blade, felt that these techniques were too difficult to master and unsuitable for widespread study. Slight modifications were made to develop a new set of entry-level techniques...

And the outcome of that was... Few were willing to give up the easily attainable for hard work, and fewer and fewer were willing to meditate and spend decades on the original teachings of Grand Master Guhua...

As time flashed by, our three ultimate techniques gradually became legendary, yet their power was ever-weakening. Our clan's vigor wound up declining and faith in it was lost... A shame. A shame, indeed...
Tell me about the Guhua Clan...
Grand Master Guhua was our founder, but not the clan's first master.

The grand master was a wandering hero who brought peace and justice to the people while traveling the world. He never intended to start a clan. This place wasn't always known as Wangshan Hall, instead being known as the "Lingmeng Chamber."

After the grand master passed away, Boxuan... better known as Master Baixuan, who once received his favor, established the Guhua Brotherhood in his name and called himself a disciple of Guhua to carry on the grand master's legacy.
So they were a self-proclaimed disciple?
Indeed. The grand master never took one.

Unfortunately, the glory of the clan faded with time. We, disciples of future generations, failed the clan. Now, we no longer seek to restore the clan's glory, merely to uphold justice and live up to the grand master's name.
And that's why the disciples of the Guhua Clan are spread all over Liyue?
Exactly. After all, there are places in the world where the sun doesn't shine, and we cannot leave everything to Rex Lapis.
But... My only consolation is a promising junior — Xingqiu. If he can restore our former glory... Then we of the older generation can die without regrets.
I want to take a look at Wangshan Hall.
...Forgive me for not accompanying you. Farewell.
Anyway, now that you've breezed through the trials, it's time to check out Wangshan Hall!
Tour Wangshan Hall with Huang the Third
Behold, the lifeblood of our clan for a millennium — Wangshan Hall!
(Is this really it? It looks like it's seen better days...)
When Grand Master Guhua founded our clan, this was a place of untold majesty where great heroes from all over gathered in merriment and endless song...
Even the realm of the adepti pales in comparison to Wangshan Hall!
Hmm... Speaking of which, what does the name mean anyway?
Haha, I'll tell you! Legend has it that when Grand Master Guhua wandered the world, his sword terrified all bandits, and the countryside boasted of being as peaceful as the city...

The mountain thieves were as frightened of Guhua as they were of tigers. The tiger is king over the beasts, and thus did the locals under the grand master's protection call this place Wangshan Hall out of respect for his supremacy over the mountains.
Wouldn't it have been called something like Mighty Tiger Mountain then?
That's super weird! Wangshan Hall sounds way cooler!
Ahem... Anyway, for a thousand years since then, the Guhua Clan has tirelessly honed its martial arts techniques here...
(Honed this place into the ground, more like...)
Come! I'll show you around. Follow me around this Guhua Sword-Honing Stone...
(Traveler, Traveler, keep your eyes peeled! Maybe we'll find some clues concerning the treasures!)
Um, Huang the Third... The way you're walking...
Haha! How embarrassing! Anyway, this is the Guhua Clan's secret movement technique — Soaring Swallow! Forgive me for not showing it to you earlier, but it's a rule of our clan...
Um, Paimon just wanted to ask... You're shaking like a leaf all of a sudden. Are you not feeling well? Or do you just need a break...
Behold! This is the arsenal of Wangshan Hall! Spears, swords, halberds... Anything you can think of, you'll find it here!
Ah. As ever, this is such a wondrous sight! Although only a few shields and spears remain, I'm sure the grand scenes of the past are already flashing before your eyes!
Speak with Huang the Third and go to the next location
What do you think? If you've seen enough of this place, shall I bring you to the next spot, Traveler?
Not so fast. I'd like to get a closer look around...
Tour Wangshan Hall with Huang the Third
Whoa, as expected of a long-standing martial arts clan...
Look, Traveler! Even the pillars here have been engraved with so many sword-wielding figures...
Huh? There seems to be a censer here.
Haha, good eye! The memorial tablet of our ancestral founder, Guhua, is enshrined here!
Our grand master traveled the world, and the only reason the clan has lasted a thousand years is because the world admires his virtue and remembers him with gratitude.
This tablet is here to remind future generations of our clan to never forget the teachings of our ancestors... To help those in need and bring peace to the people.
Speak with Huang the Third and go to the next location
What do you think? If you've seen enough of this place, shall I bring you to the next spot, Traveler?
Not so fast. I'd like to get a closer look around...
Tour Wangshan Hall with Huang the Third
Haha, this is the Arcane Archive, where all the ancient mysteries and secrets of the Guhua Clan are hidden, so only our own disciples are allowed within.
Is that so... This is as far as we go, then!
Alright! That concludes the tour of Wangshan Hall!
Huh? It's over already? What about the treasures?
Haha, treasure isn't something you just randomly find! You know, in all these centuries, many a disciple has sought out the grand master's treasures, but none have succeeded...
Oh... In that case, let's take another look around nearby!
Not so fast! If you wish to continue exploring our clan's secrets, you must follow its rules with a show of good faith.
Take Grand Master Guhua for example — in his time, disciples who wished to master spear and sword also had to pay tuition...
We agreed there'd be no fee!
Paimon's never heard of the Guhua Clan cheating people of their Mora either!
That's... We're not cheating people of anything! Ahem... We don't need Mora, exactly, but rules are rules! In Grand Master Guhua's time, it wasn't Mora he accepted, but rice and salted meat...
Huh? So, you want food, not money?
Well... *sigh* Fine, if you don't have Mora, you can just make me something to eat! That's what our predecessors did. As a great hero, surely that's within your means?

After all, we value a helping hand in times of need. It's all up to you, as long as it's from the heart. I have no intention of extorting you, Traveler — I just wouldn't want you to have a guilty conscience!
I certainly am a great hero of our times...
No, Traveler! Don't let him get to you!
And if my conscience is clear?
Hmph, you just want something to eat, don't you!? We'll make you something to eat!
Optional Dialogue: Talk to Huang the Third
I'm not sure what delicacy you've prepared, but I'll just wait over here!
Paimon seems to have an idea of some sort...
Step to the side and listen to Paimon's idea
Hmph! Didn't he say "The Guhua Clan used to accept rice and salted meat"? Then why don't we fulfill his wish, and make him some salted meat?
Seemingly fired up by the thought, the usually-lazy Paimon begins to set up a campfire all by herself...
It's supposed to be a salted dish, so it doesn't really matter how much salt we actually cover this thing with, right?
Ugh, Paimon feels a little bad, ruining perfectly good food... No, no, it's too late to feel bad about it now! And either way, it's all that guy's fault!
Now it's time to add the salt! Hehe, Paimon will leave that to you, Traveler!
This might be a bit too much...
Wh—What do you mean? Hey, if anyone's going a bit too far, wouldn't it be that guy? He tricked us into coming here with the promise of treasure, and even wanted us to cook for him for free!
Someone like that has to be taught a lesson! If he doesn't learn that what he's doing is wrong, he will just continue to scam other people once we leave this place! Isn't that right, Traveler?
There's no need to be stingy! You can add more, like this—
Yep, now that's more like it! Keep going!
Forget it, doing it like this takes too long... Why don't we just dump all the rest of the stuff in?
Woohoo! It's done! This is Paimon's specialty salted meat dish!
I could have sworn I also saw something strange get added in...
Anyway, let's bring this to Huang the Third right away!
Oh! You sure work quickly, Traveler! In just the blink of an eye, you've already prepared such a sumptuous dish!
I suppose you could call it a "sumptuous dish"...
Give Paimon's special dish to Huang the Third
Huang the Third takes a bite out of Paimon's special salted meat dish...

Before his muscles have finished masticating the first mouthful, he has already pushed the plate of meat aside, and made a mad dash to the nearby pond, where he ingurgitates several large gulps of water...
That's... That's right! No wonder they call you the Cloudpiercing Arrow, Huang the Third! We could barely keep up...
Wait, that's not the point! This is our specially-made salted meat dish, our "helping hand in times of need"! This came from our hearts, just as you asked!
Hehe, this is our "helping hand in times of need"! This came from our hearts, just as you asked!
Save it, I knew what you were up to from the start.
You were planning to pull a fast one on us as if we were ordinary tourists, weren't you?
Hmph, that's right! And Paimon bets all that talk about the treasures was just to bum a free meal! Do you really think you can outrun the Millelith?

Hold on a moment! Don't get the Millelith involved, please! I'm a martial artist and a proper, senior member of the Guhua Clan! If a man my age went to jail for scamming tourists, I'd never outlive the shame of it!
If you had any shame, you wouldn't have scammed anyone at all!
Besides, I wasn't lying... There may not be "12 Secret Chambers" in Wangshan Hall, but the relic really does exist! We modern disciples just don't know where it is.
What's the difference? Hmph! We're gonna tell the Millelith that you're a mean old man who's deliberately swindling tourists, cheating them out of their food, and... and...
Okay, okay! I've received a lot of tourists in my time here, but hardly any as honest as you two...

How about this, to repay you for not reporting me to the authorities, I'll make an exception. I'll give you the key to the library. There are countless secret martial arts books in there. Surely they also count as "treasures"?
Huang the Third produces a big set of keys...
They're all the same, they're all spare keys to the library.
Why do you have so many spares?
Haha, at my age, I'm afraid I'll lose them. Here, take this one.
But if you lost one, wouldn't you lose them all?
That's right... Wait a minute, we were just threatening to report you. Why are you suddenly giving us something so important?
True, especially if they're the genuine secret texts of the Guhua Clan...
Aren't you worried we might actually be bad people?
...To be honest, the tourists from before paid in Mora, which I used to maintain the things buried here.
Not that there's much left of the Guhua Clan now... The only ones left here who still have any desire to return and keep things running are a few of us old folks.
It's just a shame. At our age, we have the will, but not the strength... You two saw how run-down the place is. The most we can do now is keep the dust at bay.
Haha. If Grand Master Guhua were still around, I'm sure he'd laugh at our powerlessness.
But the Guhua Clan's thousand-year legacy can't just be buried along with us old folks, can it? I can tell that you're good people... and good people are hard to come by these days.
Your willingness to humor an old fool like me brought some life back into this place... The way I see it, better these books be entrusted to you than left to rot in this place.
Haha... Anyway, that's enough of this sentimental talk. Let's just say I'm giving the key to you so you won't report me.
Traveler... No, Mr./Ms. Traveler, you may stay here as long as you like. As for the books in the library... Take any that catch your eye.

Also, the whole treasure thing still turned out to be a hoax in the end! Ugh, Paimon's really mad now! Just you wait, she's going to find something super valuable to take away from the Arcane Archive today!
Sigh... To think that after a thousand years of heritage, the Guhua Clan's head doorkeeper must cheat others to survive... What shall we do, what can be done...
Ugh... Alright, come on now. Am I not sparing my junior from starving to death while guarding our gates...?
And don't look at me like that. You're always depressed... But you were pleased to see Traveler's performance just now, weren't you?
That fool from before? They get to take a look around in exchange for making you a meal? And I should be pleased about that? Were I a few years younger, I would slay you in one smack...
Heh! Your technique was inferior to mine just a few years ago!
...Anyway, listen... Only a few of us geezers are left. What do you plan to do after we stop testing those who may be destined to join us? Take those books with us to our graves?
Hah... Just forget it. What's the point of saying such depressing things?
Oh, why don't you try the food those kids made me? Hehe! I think it's better than Wanmin Restaurant's fare! Here, take a bite! Just a bite!
scene appears if the player unlocks the quest by talking to
or knocking on the Wangshan Hall door.
At Huang the Third's invitation, you arrive at the legendary place where the treasure of the Guhua Clan is located, Wangshan Hall...
Excuse me... What is this place?
If you're lost... keep following that road over there, it'll take you to Yilong Wharf.
Hmm, Wangshan Hall? Now where has Paimon heard this name before...?
Oh yeah! Paimon remembers now! ... That's the stronghold of the Guhua Clan, right!?
Wow, Paimon never expected it would be in this sort of place... Well, can we go in and look around?
Look around, you say? I didn't think outsiders would want to visit such a remote, mountainous place!
But since you're here, let me fetch one of my seniors to show you around. I'm not urbane or eloquent enough to receive visitors, I fear.
You two wait here while I go to Yilong Wharf. I won't be long!
Even before the voice of Wen the Fourth has faded, he has turned and disappeared into the forest...
Whoa! Impressive! Now you see him, now you don't!
So that's real qinggong in action, eh...
Wait, hang on a second. Did he just say Yilong Wharf!? So how long are we gonna be waiting!?
Well, seeing as he's proficient in martial arts, it probably won't take him too long to get from here to there...
Nothing for it but to wait, then.
It is not long before Wen the Fourth returns with another person...
Hey, c'mon, a bit slower, will you? These old bones can't keep up with your pace...
...I AM going slow. And let's be real, you're the one who's neglected your skills for so long that you've put on all this meat...
Enough, that's enough. Don't say such things in front of visitors!
Er, and this old gentleman is...?
Huang the Third of the Guhua Clan. Some people know me as the Cloudpiercing Arrow.
Cloudpiercing Arrow...? So you're a talented archer?
Haha, it was just a moniker bestowed on me in my youth. Pay it no mind.
Hmph, what do you mean "bestowed upon"? You just call yourself that...
Why don't you just keep a lid on it? Ahem... Anyway, you'd like to visit Wangshan Hall, right?
Haha, very good! With your skills, perhaps you might find the treasures left by Grand Master Guhua!
Quite so! Grand Master Guhua secretly established 12 Secret Chambers within the Hall upon the Clan's founding, in which he placed treasures now lost to the world.
12 Secret Chambers, eh? That's very specific of you. Did you take the time to count them yourself...?

Wangshan Hall is hewn from the mountain stone itself, and its structure is complex and intricate. Over millennia, only a handful of these hidden chambers have been discovered. The locations of many others are unknown even to us disciples.
Actually, we were just passing through...
I see... Alas! Would it not be a shame if the treasures housed still within Wangshan Hall will remain thus neglected, deep within the mountains...! A shame. Such a shame!
Quite so! Grand Master Guhua secretly established 12 Secret Chambers within the Hall upon the Clan's founding, in which he placed treasures now lost to the world.

Wangshan Hall is hewn from the mountain stone itself, and its structure is complex and intricate. Over millennia, only a handful of these hidden chambers have been discovered. The locations of many others are unknown even to us disciples.
Oh... Even we disciples don't know where they are, and you're bringing outsiders in?
Brother, you... Calm yourself. Stop interrupting me!
I sense something special about you. Perchance you might stumble across one of Grand Master Guhua's hidden chambers during your visit to this historical site!
(This has to be a gimmick to sucker tourists in...)
(Surely no one could fall for this...)
For real!? Traveler, Traveler! Let's go see those secret Guhua Clan treasures, shall we?
Hey, what's with that look? Aren't you tempted at all? We'll be rich if we find that forgotten treasure!
Oh! That reminds Paimon... Isn't Xingqiu a member of the Guhua Clan? Maybe he once trained here!
Fine then. Lead the way, Paimon.
Hehe, awesome! Paimon just knew that you'd be interested!
Hang on, how much does it cost to tour Wangshan Hall?
Cost? Haha, surely you jest! We are honorable disciples of the Guhua Clan, and wish only to invite those of character to come visit. We are not brash, swindling charlatans!
I'm not even asking for a penny! How about it? Would you care to come with me for a tour, and together explore our clan's illustrious history?
Great, that's just what I was thinking!
Since it's free, we might as well go take a look...
However, Wangshan Hall is, in the end, very important to us. Custom dictates, therefore, that outsiders who wish to enter must first pass two trials.
Put another way, we first wish to see just how skilled you are!
Of course, of course! These two tests will certainly be a walk in the park for your illustrious selves!
It's just that these are the rules of our clan, and we can't simply change them arbitrarily. Please forgive me.
Haha, no need to worry! These two tests will certainly be a walk in the park for your illustrious selves!
It's just that these are the rules of our clan, and we can't simply change them arbitrarily. Please forgive me.
Boo... Nothing for it. Guess we've gotta do it for Guhua's treasure...
So, what exactly do I need to do?
It's very simple. Please, follow me!
Outsiders who seek to enter Wangshan Hall must pass two trials. This rule has existed since ancient times.
However... Forget it, if my senior thinks it's all right, then I have nothing to say about it. Anyway, there's nothing to worry about. Anyone can pass the trials as they are right now.
...You will understand soon enough. Go on, don't let me keep you. My senior is waiting.
We'll be on our way then. Goodbye!
Go on, my senior is waiting.
